name: combat-ui-pattern-a
description: Implement Split-Panel Combat UI (Pattern A) for SHINOBI WAY game. Use when user wants to create the horizontal confrontation combat layout, character panels, action dock, phase header, VS divider, or any component from the Pattern A combat UI system. Guides through component creation following the established architecture.
capabilities: Generate TypeScript React components with proper interfaces for combat UI
Create CSS Grid layouts for split-panel combat design with responsive breakpoints
Define Tailwind CSS styling tokens and color palettes for consistent theming
Implement CSS keyframe animations for combat feedback effects
Build component hierarchy from CombatLayout down to individual action cards
Follow migration strategy from theater mode to split-panel confrontation layout
author: CesarAugustusGroB
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only TypeScript interface definitions, CSS/Tailwind styling tokens, animation specifications, and implementation workflows for UI component development. All 263 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by pattern-matching markdown code blocks and Tailwind color class names without contextual understanding.
valueStatement: Game developers building combat interfaces need consistent, well-architected UI components. This skill provides complete implementation guidance including TypeScript interfaces, CSS Grid layouts, animation specifications, and responsive styling tokens for the Pattern A combat UI system.
limitations: Requires existing React + TypeScript project setup
Does not include actual implementation code files, provides documentation only
Depends on game types from @/game/types imports for type definitions
Designed specifically for SHINOBI WAY game architecture patterns

useCases.title: Build combat interface
useCases.description: Create horizontal split-panel combat UI with player and enemy panels for confrontation gameplay

useCases.title: Design action dock
useCases.description: Implement skill bar with quick actions (SIDE/TOGGLE) and main action cards (MAIN) following design system

useCases.title: Add combat animations
useCases.description: Add floating text, screen shake, and character attack animations with timing guidelines

promptTemplates.title: Create CombatLayout
promptTemplates.prompt: Create CombatLayout component for Pattern A split-panel combat UI following the CSS Grid structure in layout-specs.md

promptTemplates.title: Build CharacterPanel
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement CharacterPanel component with player and enemy variants using the interfaces from component-interfaces.md

promptTemplates.title: Add animations
promptTemplates.prompt: Add floating text and screen shake animations following the keyframe definitions in animations.md

promptTemplates.title: Style with tokens
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply styling tokens from styling-tokens.md to create consistent color palette and spacing for combat components

bestPractices: Use TypeScript interfaces from component-interfaces.md for type safety across all components
Apply styling tokens consistently from styling-tokens.md to maintain design system cohesion
Implement responsive breakpoints with mobile-first approach (stacked layout below 768px)
Follow the four-phase migration strategy: Layout Foundation, Component Extraction, Visual Polish, Cleanup
antiPatterns: Hardcoding colors instead of using defined styling tokens from the design system
Ignoring the mobile fallback layout (stacked) for tablet and larger split-panel designs
Skipping animation timing guidelines (150ms micro-interactions, 400ms attack animations)
Not following the component hierarchy from CombatLayout down to individual action cards

faq.question: What React version is required?
faq.answer: React 18+ with TypeScript support. Component patterns use React.FC type conventions.

faq.question: What screen sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Mobile under 640px (stacked) to desktop 1280px+ (full split). Breakpoints at 640px, 768px, 1024px, and 1280px.

faq.question: Can this work with other UI frameworks?
faq.answer: Styling tokens use Tailwind CSS conventions. Component patterns are React-specific but CSS Grid concepts are adaptable.

faq.question: Is any data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: No data storage or network transmission. Pure guidance skill providing documentation and code examples for user implementation.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing code?
faq.answer: Reference existing components at src/components/PlayerHUD.tsx and src/scenes/Combat.tsx for props mapping and state connections.

faq.question: How is Pattern A different from other layouts?
faq.answer: Pattern A implements horizontal split-panel layout for player vs enemy confrontation. It replaces vertical theater mode with side-by-side panels and action dock.
